_comment: Declarative OpenRouter BYOK (bring-your-own-key) provider credentials. Sync with `just openrouter-byok-sync` (tools/openrouter_byok.py). BYOK lets OpenRouter route a model to a provider's first-party endpoint using YOUR key + credits (OpenRouter takes a ~5% fee). Once a credential exists, OpenRouter auto-prioritizes it for that provider — no per-request pinning required (pin with provider.only:[<slug>] to force it). NOTE: the BYOK API (`POST /api/v1/byok`) requires an OpenRouter MANAGEMENT/provisioning key, NOT a normal sk-or- key. Set management_key_op (or env OPENROUTER_MGMT_KEY). Provider keys are read from 1Password at sync time; nothing secret is stored here. Alternative: add these in the dashboard at https://openrouter.ai/settings/integrations .
